title: "We can break even and be self-sustaining within Riot" – The firm behind League of Legends is "super close" to making esports sustainable
summary: "We can break even and be self-sustaining within Riot" – The firm behind League of Legends is "super close" to making esports sustainable &nbs...
content:
Riot Games' publishing and esports boss, John Needham, on the journey to profitability, Riot's transmedia ambitions, and why it allowed gambling sponsorship

Riot Games' esports business still isn't profitable.

This has been the case for a long time, and historically it hasn'tnecessarilybeen a problem for the League of Legends giant; at various points in the past, then-head of esports John Needham explained that his firmwanted to ensure the broader ecosystem, including the various esports teams, was profitable. But the aim was for esports to be a sustainable business in its own right.

That business is now "super close" to being profitable. What's changed? Needham –now head of publishing and esports at Riot– says the firm has adjusted how it operates its pro-gaming business since 2020.

"Coming out of the pandemic, we were in this esports winter," he tellsGamesIndustry.biz.

"The sponsorship business was weak, teams were having a hard time getting capital and investment in their business. We pivoted our business model away from sponsorships to digital items; that was a big positive move for teams. Teams also adjusted how much they were spending. The team business is in as good a place as it has ever been, now, and we are trying to do other things to allow teams to make alternate sources of revenue."

title: LoL Esports |
content:
We’re delighted that we can finally reveal that the culmination to the LEC season, the LEC Summer Finals, is coming to Nice, France!

Which team will earn the final spot up for grabs at Worlds this year and take a shot at international glory? The answer awaits in Nice.

We will be welcoming our passionate community to the Palais Nikaia in Nice from September 18 to 20. The capital of the French Riviera will be taken over by the top LEC teams and players, as well as your favourite broadcast talent, co-streamers and of course, thousands of our beloved LEC fans ready to cheer on their team. The three-day League of Legends extravaganza will be an unforgettable experience.

Here’s everything you need to know about the most exciting weekend in the LEC calendar!

The entire season is building up to this moment as we not only crown our Summer champion but also find out who will secure the third and final spot to represent the LEC at World’s in North America in October.

Last year’s Summer Finals in Madrid was spectacular! The fans, the show, and the energy, were truly next level. We’re excited to do it all again while continuing to evolve and deliver the best fan experience possible.

title: Bilibili Gaming surge into Mid-Season Invitational final
summary: Bilibili Gaming surge into Mid-Season Invitational final  Field Level Media
content:
Bilibili Gaming defeated Hanwha Life 3-1 on Thursday to reserve their spot in the League of Legends Mid-Season Invitational grand final in Daejeon, South Korea.

Hanwha Life slid to the lower bracket final and will meet the winner of the third-round match between G2 Esports and LYON, to be played Friday.

Bilibili opened with a 25-minute victory on blue and a 29-minute win on red before Hanwha Life triumphed in 31 minutes on blue. Bilibili closed it out in 38 minutes on red.

Ding “Knight” Zhuo of Bilibili was the MVP of the match. The 26-year-old, who is from China, led his team with a 25-3-29 kills-deaths-assists ratio.

The grand final of the $2 million tournament will be played on Monday. The champion will receive $500,000 and qualify for this fall’s 2026 World Championship. All matches are best-of-five.

2026 Mid-Season Invitational prize pool:1. $500,0002. $300,0003. $240,0004. $200,0005-6. $160,000 — T1, Secret Whales7-8. $110,000 — FURIA, Top Esports9. $90,000 — Team Liquid10. $70,000 — Karmine Corp.11. $60,000 — Deep Cross Gaming

title: Most-Watched Esports Teams H1 2026: T1, Falcons, Alter Ego
summary: Most-Watched Esports Teams H1 2026: T1, Falcons, Alter Ego  Esports Charts
content:
LIVE Viewership Updates & Audience Trends

The first half of the year saw two clear frontrunners in terms of the most-watched PC esports team by Hours Watched (HW). League of Legends' most popular side, T1, and the current flavor of the Counter-Strike world, Team Falcons, put up almost similar numbers, but it was the Korean giant who edged this battle.

Nevertheless, Team Falcons' incredible run to a maiden major title helped Counter-Strikeset multiple new viewership records. The tournament triumph also helped the Saudi Arabia-based esports organization finally end its long streak of disappointing results at major events.

Most-watched PC esports teams in H1 2026

T1continued its reign as the undisputed kings ofLeague of Legendsesports viewership. Between January and June 2026, the defending three-time world champion roster accrued 65.28 million watch hours across a remarkable five tournaments, with most of this total coming from regional events like theLCK 2026 Seasonand theLCK Cup 2026.

T1 also remained the game's most popular side, as the all-Korean showdown between T1 andGen.Greached 2.27 million Peak Viewers (PV) on the final day of theLCK 2026 Road to MSIqualifier. T1 won this hard-fought clash 3:2 to secure the last available spot at theMid-Season Invitational 2026and ensure Gen.G failed to qualify, thus missing out on the opportunity to defend its title on home soil.

title: These iconic moments prove why LoL's Mid-Season Invitational matters
summary: These iconic moments prove why LoL's Mid-Season Invitational matters  Red Bull
content:
The competitive League of Legends ecosystem is buzzing following the latest announcement regarding 'These iconic moments prove why LoL's Mid-Season Invitational matters'. As the season progresses, this development has quickly taken center stage, drawing intense scrutiny from fans, analysts, and professional players alike. The core update highlights a significant shift that is expected to have wide-ranging consequences for the regional leagues and international tournaments.

In Europe's LEC, the atmosphere is electric as teams prepare to integrate these updates into their drafts. G2 Esports and Fnatic have historically led the charge in innovative pocket picks and creative map rotations. This new development could provide the perfect opportunity for rising rosters to challenge the established order and secure crucial championship points for the upcoming international events.

Speaking on the recent events, seasoned esports analysts noted that 'These iconic moments prove why LoL's Mid-Season Invitational matters  Red Bull' is not just a temporary trend but a fundamental shift in how teams will approach their upcoming matchdays. 'Draft priority and map control are going to be completely redefined over the next few weeks,' remarked a prominent league caster. 'Teams that fail to recognize the gravity of this change will find themselves falling behind very quickly in the standings.'

As the community awaits the upcoming weekend matches, anticipation continues to build. Fans can catch all the high-stakes action live on official streaming channels, complete with expert desk analysis and live regional broadcasts. With so much on the line, the next chapter of the 2026 competitive season promises to deliver some of the most memorable moments in League of Legends esports history.
